mapping.b_element: magnetization / order parameter of the Blume-Capel spin model

mapping.a_element: first-order vs second-order character of the transition
mapping.b_element: first-order vs second-order transition regimes of the pure model

mapping.a_element: temperature (and quark chemical potential) as control parameters
mapping.b_element: temperature (and bond randomness) as control parameters

brief_justification: Both study phase transitions with order parameters, but the transferable machinery differs: paper A minimizes a mean-field grand-canonical effective potential to obtain a QCD deconfinement/chiral phase diagram, whereas paper B runs Wang-Landau entropic-sampling Monte Carlo with finite-size scaling to extract critical exponents and universality classes under quenched bond disorder. The only overlap is the generic Landau concept of order parameters and first/second-order transitions, not the same governing process, so no role-for-role method mapping holds (A has no analogue of Wang-Landau sampling/universality-class analysis; B has no analogue of the effective-potential minimization or Polyakov-loop dynamics).
